# Feature Flags

This codebase uses build-time feature flags heavily. That is a major part of the architecture.

## Build-Time Gating

`main.tsx`, `tools.ts`, and many tool or command modules use `bun:bundle` `feature()` checks to remove code from the final bundle. This is not just runtime branching; it is dead-code elimination.

## Runtime Flags

There are also environment-based runtime flags for things like:
- user type
- remote mode
- background tasks
- coordinator mode
- assistant mode
- task visibility

## Why It Is Structured This Way

This separation lets the same codebase produce different products or internal modes without splitting into separate repos. It also keeps the shipped bundle smaller by omitting feature-gated code paths entirely when the gate is off.

## Practical Implication

When documenting behavior, always note whether it is:
- always on
- env-gated
- feature-gated at build time
- limited to a specific user type

That distinction matters because two sessions on the same codebase can expose very different command and tool surfaces.
